introduction:
A Caste Certificate (also called a Community Certificate) is an official document issued by the Maharashtra Government that certifies a person's Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), Vimukta Jati (VJ), Nomadic Tribe (NT), Special Backward Class (SBC), or Other Backward Class (OBC) status.

It is essential for availing government reservations in education, employment, government schemes, and elections. In Maharashtra, it is issued by the Sub-Divisional Officer (SDO) / Tehsildar / District Collector's office based on where the applicant's family originally resides.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Caste Certificate is issued based on father's / ancestral permanent address, not current residence
  • Processing time: 15–30 working days
  • Fee: ₹20 (approx.) — varies by district
  • Certificate is valid for lifetime for SC/ST; OBC certificates may need renewal
  • Online applications available via Aaple Sarkar Portal
  • Caste Validity Certificate (CVC) required separately for higher education and jobs
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

Identity & Address Proof
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID Card
  • Passport
  • Ration Card
  • School Leaving Certificate
Caste Proof (Ancestral)
  • Father's / Grandfather's Caste Certificate (if available)
  • School Leaving Certificate mentioning caste
  • Revenue / Village records (7/12 Extract)
  • Birth Certificate mentioning caste
Other Documents
  • Income Certificate (for OBC Non-Creamy Layer)
  • Self-declaration affidavit (if no old caste proof)
  • Domicile Certificate
  • Passport-size photographs (2)
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via Aaple Sarkar

Maharashtra's Aaple Sarkar Portal allows citizens to apply for Caste Certificate from home.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Portal
    Go to aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in
  2. Register / Login
    New users: Click 'New User Registration' and register using your mobile number and Aadhaar. Existing users: Login with your credentials.
  3. Search for Service
    Search for 'Caste Certificate' in the services list or navigate to Revenue Department → Caste Certificate.
  4. Fill Application Form
    Enter personal details: name, date of birth, permanent address (ancestral village address is critical), caste/tribe name, and sub-caste.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies (PDF/JPG) of all required documents — identity proof, ancestral caste proof, and photographs.
  6. Pay Fee Online
    Pay approximately ₹20 via net banking, debit/credit card, or UPI.
  7. Submit & Get Acknowledgement
    Submit the application and note the Application Reference Number for tracking.
  8. Track Application
    Track status at Aaple Sarkar using the reference number.
Note: Officer may call for a field verification visit. Keep original documents ready.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Tehsil / SDO Office

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Tehsildar Office
    Go to the Tehsildar / Sub-Divisional Officer's office of your ancestral village's taluka.
  2. Collect Application Form
    Obtain the prescribed Caste Certificate application form from the office counter (usually free).
  3. Fill the Form
    Fill all details carefully — name, father's name, caste, sub-caste, permanent ancestral address, district, and taluka.
  4. Attach Documents
    Attach self-attested photocopies of all required documents along with the filled form.
  5. Submit at the Counter
    Submit the completed form and pay the requisite fee (₹20 approx.) at the cashier counter.
  6. Field Verification
    The Revenue Inspector (Talathi) will conduct a field enquiry / village visit for verification of caste and ancestral residence.
  7. Collect Certificate
    After verification, collect your Caste Certificate from the Tehsildar office, usually within 15–30 working days.
Processing Time: 15–30 working days after field verification.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Your Application

Visit Aaple Sarkar Portal and enter your Application Reference Number to track the status.

Caste Validity Certificate (CVC)

After receiving the Caste Certificate, apply for a Caste Validity Certificate at the Scrutiny Committee office — mandatory for government jobs and higher education admissions.

Grievance / Delay

If delayed beyond 30 days, raise a complaint at Maharashtra Grievance Portal or contact the district collector's office.
